condition_specific: Leukemia, lymphoma, mesothelioma, multiple myeloma; cancer of brain, digestive tract, urinary tract, liver, skin, breast, cervix, thyroid, prostate, testicle; cancer with higher firefighter incidence linked to known carcinogen
notes: Three statutory mechanisms: (1) § 21-5-705 provides death benefit up to $150,000 for firefighters killed in line of duty from covered cancers after Jan 1, 2012 — firefighter must be under 68; Firefighter Benefit Review Panel makes recommendations to Arkansas State Claims Commission. (2) § 24-4-511 (APERS) and § 24-10-607 (LOPFI) provide disability retirement presumption for cancer diagnosed 'while in the line of duty.' Both rebuttable by preponderance. § 24-4-511 requires 5 years actual service including 18 of last 24 months; members hired after July 1, 2013 under § 24-10-607 require 10 years. Firefighter must not have been diagnosed with cancer prior to service start. LOPFI section also covers volunteer members.

condition_specific: Leukemia, lymphoma, mesothelioma, multiple myeloma; cancer of brain, urinary tract, liver, skin, breast, cervix, thyroid, prostate, testicle, colon, digestive tract; cancer with higher firefighter incidence linked to known carcinogen
notes: Both APERS (§ 24-4-511) and LOPFI (§ 24-10-607) explicitly extend cancer presumption to 'volunteer firefighter' / 'volunteer member.' Rebuttable by preponderance. Cancer must be diagnosed 'while in the line of duty.'
